team.detail: Roles are well-chosen. Documentation Lead at full allocation drives execution, Senior Auditors handle cleanup and verification, AI Engineer manages automated indexing/cross-referencing tools, Component Senior integrates component workpapers, and EP/EM provide review and sign-off. For a Big 4 on Apple, archival might involve more junior staff for physical/manual file assembly, but the AI-first model automates much of that work, making the lean team defensible. Total ~90 human hours for archival on a $416B engagement is tight but plausible with AI handling indexing and cross-reference verification.

allocation.detail: Engagement Partner at 0.75 allocation exceeds the typical partner governance range (0.05-0.25). However, the workflow is only 0.5 weeks, yielding 15 absolute hours—a reasonable amount of partner time for final review and sign-off of the complete audit file on a $416B engagement. The high allocation rate is a mechanical artifact of the short duration rather than a substantive concern. All other allocations are plausible: Doc Lead at 1.0 (20h, full execution), Senior Auditors at 0.75 each (30h combined for cleanup), AI Engineer at 0.5 (10h), EM at 0.375 (7.5h), Component Senior at 0.375 (7.5h).

description.detail: AS 1215 (Audit Documentation) is the correct PCAOB standard for this workflow. The 14-day assembly deadline per AS 1215.16 is accurate. The 7-year retention requirement attributed to SOX Section 802 is substantively correct (SOX 802 establishes criminal penalties for destruction; the 7-year period derives from SEC Rule 2-06 implementing SOX 802). However, the parenthetical hour breakdown is inconsistent with the allocation math. Computed hours: Partner review ~15h (not 30h), team cleanup/verification ~65h (not 40h), AI-assisted archival execution ~10h (not 20h). The total of 90h is correct but the category breakdown is wrong.

requirements.detail: documentation_completion and documentation_retention are the two directly applicable requirement_ids. documentation_completion maps to AS 1215.15-16 (completing assembly of the final engagement file). documentation_retention maps to AS 1215.14 and SOX 802/SEC Rule 2-06 (7-year retention). No other requirement_ids from the valid registry are missing—this workflow is post-opinion archival, not substantive audit work.

timing.detail: Weeks 22.5-23 (0.5 weeks) follows opinion_and_filing (weeks 22-22.5) correctly. Archival must begin after the report release date per AS 1215.16, and the input dependency on AuditOpinion enforces this sequencing. The 0.5-week duration is tight but viable given AI-automated indexing and cross-reference checking. The workflow runs concurrently with the tail end of statutory_reporting (weeks 20-23), which is appropriate as component/statutory workpapers feed into the consolidated archive.

corrections.current: Partner review (30h), team cleanup (40h), archival execution (20h).
corrections.proposed: Partner review (15h), team cleanup (65h), AI-assisted archival execution (10h).
corrections.reason: The allocation math yields: EP 1×0.75×0.5wk×40h=15h (not 30h); Doc Lead 20h + Senior Auditors 30h + EM 7.5h + Component Senior 7.5h = 65h (not 40h); AI Engineer 1×0.5×0.5wk×40h=10h (not 20h). Total remains 90h but the category breakdown must match the staffing model.
